purpose: Convert every externally blocked health, disability-rights, crisis, and educational support source into a reviewable evidence package without weakening its publication block.
publication_rule: No item in this register may be rendered as approved, expert-reviewed, clinically reviewed, legally reviewed, or publishable until all required reviewer roles are satisfied, the evidence record is complete, conflicts are disclosed, requested changes are resolved, and the source file review_status is changed in a separate audited commit.

required_reviewer_roles.minimum_basis: Licensed or institutionally credentialed professional with documented practice in child development, ADHD, learning difficulties, or educational psychology.
Documented professional experience designing or implementing inclusive educational supports and reasonable adjustments.
A person with relevant lived experience or a representative of an organization of persons with disabilities, with scope limited to dignity, agency, language, accessibility, and unintended harm.
Professional Arabic editorial competence with ability to verify clarity, non-stigmatizing language, age appropriateness, and meaning preservation.

authoritative_review_sources.title: Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder: diagnosis and management — NG87 recommendations
review_focus: family and school involvement with consent
distinguishing ADHD from coexisting learning difficulties

authoritative_review_sources.title: Guidance on Including Children with Disabilities — Education Kit Handbook
Tips on communicating with children with disabilities

acceptance_criteria: Every recommendation is educational and supportive rather than diagnostic, therapeutic, medication-related, or an eligibility determination.
Examples do not require eye contact, forced compliance, disclosure of diagnosis, public behavior charts, or loss of privacy.
Student voice, assent, refusal signals, communication access, and individual preferences are represented.
The material distinguishes ADHD-related needs from learning difficulties and other possible causes without making a diagnosis.
Environmental modifications are individualized, observable, reversible, and reviewed for benefit or unintended harm.
Family-school tracking minimizes data, avoids labels and blame, and defines who can access records and why.
Safeguarding, pain, sleep, sensory, communication, task difficulty, and medical concerns are not misclassified as deliberate noncompliance.
The Arabic text is clear across the stated age groups and does not overpromise outcomes.
Any country-specific education law, accommodation entitlement, or professional title is excluded unless separately reviewed against a current official local source.

required_reviewer_roles.minimum_basis: Authorized representative or documented contributor from an organization led by persons with disabilities, or equivalent independent lived-experience reviewer.
Documented expertise in disability rights, safeguarding, consent, supported decision-making, and applicable human-rights standards.
Licensed or institutionally credentialed professional experienced in safeguarding children or adults at risk and in recognizing medical, communication, sensory, and environmental causes of distress.
Credentialed expertise in non-directive genetic information, reproductive autonomy, uncertainty, and avoidance of life-value judgments.

authoritative_review_sources.title: QualityRights materials for training, guidance and transformation
review_focus: legal capacity and the right to decide
freedom from coercion violence and abuse

authoritative_review_sources.title: Strategies to end seclusion and restraint
Convention on the Rights of the Child text
review_focus: protection from abuse and degrading treatment

authoritative_review_sources.title: How can we achieve disability inclusion?

acceptance_criteria: The policy centers agency, participation, accessibility, supported decision-making, and individual communication preferences.
No clause normalizes forced compliance, routine seclusion, deprivation, humiliation, restraint, or coercion as a support strategy.
Safeguarding escalation distinguishes immediate protection from punishment, behavior control, or institutional convenience.
Medical, pain, medication, sleep, sensory, communication, trauma, and environmental causes are considered before behavioral hypotheses.
Consent, assent, refusal, privacy, and media use rules are implementable for children and adults with different communication access needs.
Genetic and reproductive content is non-directive, uncertainty-aware, and separated from legal or value judgments.
Terms such as abuse, restraint, seclusion, consent, assent, capacity, and emergency are either operationally defined or explicitly delegated to local reviewed policy.
Country-specific legal conclusions are excluded unless reviewed against current official local law.
Residual disagreements among reviewers are recorded rather than hidden.

required_reviewer_roles.minimum_basis: Licensed or institutionally credentialed clinician with documented crisis, suicide-prevention, emergency mental-health, or self-harm experience.
Person authorized or demonstrably qualified to verify emergency, crisis, child-protection, and violence-response routes for each intended country or jurisdiction.
Documented child and adult safeguarding expertise, including escalation, confidentiality limits, and mandatory-reporting boundaries where applicable.
Documented expertise in accessible crisis interfaces, data minimization, emergency content design, and safe wording under distress.

authoritative_review_sources.title: Suicide — questions and answers
review_focus: talking with a person at risk
not leaving a person alone when danger is immediate

acceptance_criteria: Immediate safety actions appear before explanatory content and do not require account creation, form completion, or test results.
The platform never claims to assess an individual's clinical risk, diagnose a crisis, guarantee safety, or replace emergency evaluation.
No phone number, chat, service, partner, availability schedule, cost claim, or confidentiality claim is published without a direct current official source for the exact jurisdiction.
Contact changes receive a second independent verification and every service has verified_at, review_due_at, source authority, scope, language, cost, availability, and uncertainty disclosures.
Expired, revoked, rejected, or overdue entries cannot render publicly and are removed from discovery surfaces without erasing the audit trail.
The wording covers immediate danger, minors, suspected abuse or exploitation, inability to maintain safety, and fallback to local emergency services without inventing a universal number.
Data collection is avoided by default; when unavoidable, the purpose, retention, access, deletion, and emergency limitations are visible before input.
Keyboard, screen-reader, mobile, low-literacy, and high-distress usability are tested with documented results.
Country-specific legal duties and confidentiality limits receive separate current local review.
